name: swarmforge-vc
description: Visual front-end for SwarmForge multi-agent orchestration

swarmforge.targetPath.description: Path to the target project for SwarmForge runs.

swarmforge.configPath.description: Path to the swarm config file (e.g. swarmforge/packs/seven-pack.conf). Overrides swarmforge/swarmforge.conf when launching. SWARMFORGE_CONFIG env var takes precedence.

swarmforge.agentCommand.description: Reserved for future standalone orchestrator mode. Launch Swarm uses ./swarm and swarmforge/swarmforge.conf.

swarmforge.agentArgs.description: Reserved for future standalone orchestrator mode. Agent CLI flags belong in swarmforge/swarmforge.conf.

swarmforge.roles.description: Reserved for future standalone orchestrator mode. Active roles come from swarmforge/swarmforge.conf via ./swarm.

swarmforge.run.promptForName.description: Show a run-name prompt before launching. When false, launches immediately with a generated default name.

swarmforge.comms.chaseIntervalSeconds.description: How often (seconds) the InboxChaser sweeps each role's inbox/new/ for stale handoffs.

swarmforge.comms.chaseTimeoutSeconds.description: How old (seconds) a handoff must be before the chaser re-notifies the recipient.

swarmforge.comms.maxChases.description: Maximum number of chase wake-ups before a handoff is dead-lettered.

swarmforge.comms.stuckInProcessTimeoutSe.description: How long (seconds) an item may stay in inbox/in_process before the reconciler nudges the owner.

swarmforge.tile.historyLines.description: Number of lines of scrollback history to display in agent tiles (1–50000). Default 5000.

swarmforge.tile.paneRows.description: Height in rows of each agent's tmux window, i.e. how many lines a tile can show at once (headless tmux otherwise caps at 24). 1–1000. Default 200.

swarmforge.notify.email.to.description: Recipient address for needs-human email notifications (Resend). The feature is off until this and a Resend API key (RESEND_API_KEY env var, or the swarmforge.resendApiKey secret) are both configured.

swarmforge.notify.email.from.description: Sender address for needs-human email notifications. The Resend onboarding sender only delivers to the account owner; set a verified domain address here once you have one.

swarmforge.notify.email.graceSeconds.description: How long (seconds) a role's needs-human state must persist unanswered before an email notification is sent.

swarmforge.notify.email.cooldownSeconds.description: Minimum time (seconds) between two email notifications for the same role.

swarmforge.bounce.drainTimeoutSeconds.description: How long (seconds) a graceful bounce (SwarmForge: Bounce Gracefully) waits for every role to go idle before prompting the human to keep waiting or bounce now.

swarmforge.contextClear.enabled.description: Send /clear to a role's pane once it has been drained-idle (no work held or queued, no pending question, no recent human input) through the settle window, so the next parcel starts with a fresh context window.

swarmforge.contextClear.settleWindowSeco.description: How long (seconds) a role must stay drained-idle and output-quiet, with no recent human keystrokes, before its pane is cleared.

swarmforge.contextClear.fullnessThreshol.description: Minimum context-window fullness (0-100) required, in addition to the drained-idle settle window, before a role's pane is cleared. When the backend does not report real context usage, a deterministic pane-history proxy metric is used instead, and the Context Clear output channel labels each clear it drives as proxy mode.

swarmforge.metrics.suiteWarnSeconds.description: The METRICS pane and swarm-metrics CLI flag the suite-duration entry as WARN when the latest test run exceeds this many seconds (or twice the rolling mean, whichever is stricter).

scripts.mutation: npm run compile && node scripts/ensureStrykerSandboxSiblings.js && stryker run
